commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=69ca0e22c47f5c44859bae42c13d16f0e6c9306d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a bundled `gigachat` provider plugin with catalog/onboarding metadata, OAuth runtime token exchange, docs, labeler coverage, plugin inventory entries, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new provider feature, not a bug report. The code path is reviewable from the PR head and current main, but the PR body only reports mocked/runtime tests, not live GigaChat OAuth/chat behavior.
commands.review_followup: Keep in normal human PR review: the branch is draft, needs contributor real behavior proof, and the endpoint-class/product decision should be handled on the PR.

target.title: feat(gigachat): add provider integration
target.body:
# feat(gigachat): add provider integration

## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw did not have a bundled GigaChat provider, and GigaChat cannot be represented as a plain static API-key provider because Sber requires OAuth token exchange from an Authorization key.
- Why it matters: Users with GigaChat access need a first-class provider path that preserves OpenClaw auth profiles, TLS/proxy request policy, model catalog, docs, and failover behavior.
- What changed: Added the bundled `gigachat` provider plugin with static text model catalog, Authorization-key onboarding, scoped OAuth runtime token exchange, endpoint selection, docs, labeler coverage, generated plugin reference updates, and focused tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Did not add GigaChat image/file/audio adapters, tool/function calling support, dynamic model discovery, live credential migration, or broad core provider behavior changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only. Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes; terminal screenshots and copied live output count. Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

- Behavior addressed: Adds GigaChat provider registration, onboarding metadata, catalog defaults, OAuth runtime auth exchange, docs, and labeler/docs navigation coverage.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS Darwin 25.3.0 source checkout on branch `gigachat-provider`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/gigachat/index.test.ts extensions/gigachat/runtime-auth.test.ts`; `node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check`; `git diff --check`; `scripts/committer "feat(gigachat): add provider integration" ...`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Targeted Vitest output reported `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 11 passed (11)`. Plugin inventory check and `git diff --check` exited successfully. Commit hook formatted staged files and created `69ca0e22c4 feat(gigachat): add provider integration`.
- Observed result after fix: The provider plugin registers, exposes the expected auth choice and catalog, applies onboarding defaults, selects the business endpoint from plugin config, classifies GigaChat rate/context errors, and exchanges Authorization keys for Bearer tokens in mocked runtime-auth tests.
- What was not tested: Live GigaChat OAuth/chat request was not tested because `GIGACHAT_AUTHORIZATION_KEY` was not present in env or `~/.profile`, and no trusted GigaChat root certificate path was available. Broad Testbox/Crabbox changed gate was not run because `crabbox`/`blacksmith` were unavailable in this environment.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): N/A for a new provider surface.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: N/A.
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/gigachat/runtime-auth.test.ts`; `extensions/gigachat/index.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: Runtime OAuth request headers/body, scope selection, Basic-prefix normalization, TLS/private-network request passthrough, token cache/deduplication, provider auth metadata, static catalog, endpoint selection, onboarding defaults, and failover/context classification.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests cover the provider-owned seams without requiring real Sber credentials or changing shared core provider contracts.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A.
- If no new test is added, why not: New focused tests were added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

OpenClaw now includes a bundled `gigachat` provider with auth choice `gigachat-authorization-key`, env var `GIGACHAT_AUTHORIZATION_KEY`, default model `gigachat/GigaChat-2`, optional plugin config `scope`, and optional endpoint selection between `main` and `business`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[user has Sber Authorization key] -> [no bundled GigaChat provider] -> [manual/custom config only]

After:
[user runs GigaChat onboarding] -> [Authorization key stored] -> [runtime OAuth exchange] -> [Bearer token chat request]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The new provider stores the long-lived Sber Authorization key through the existing provider auth profile path and only keeps short-lived access tokens in runtime memory/cache. OAuth calls use OpenClaw provider HTTP helpers for timeout, SSRF, proxy, and TLS handling, and protected OAuth headers cannot be overridden by configured request headers.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS Darwin 25.3.0
- Runtime/container: Local source checkout; Node-based Vitest wrapper
- Model/provider: `gigachat/GigaChat-2`, `gigachat/GigaChat-2-Pro`, `gigachat/GigaChat-2-Max`
- Integration/channel (if any): GigaChat provider plugin
- Relevant config (redacted): `GIGACHAT_AUTHORIZATION_KEY` expected for live auth; not present during local verification

### Steps

1. Add the GigaChat plugin package, manifest, provider entry, onboarding, catalog, runtime auth, docs, and labeler/docs navigation updates.
2. Run focused provider tests with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/gigachat/index.test.ts extensions/gigachat/runtime-auth.test.ts`.
3. Run generated docs check with `node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check`.
4. Run whitespace check with `git diff --check`.

### Expected

- GigaChat provider registration, catalog, onboarding defaults, endpoint selection, OAuth exchange, and error classification tests pass.

### Actual

- Focused tests passed: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 11 passed (11)`.
- Generated plugin inventory check passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Live GigaChat smoke and broad remote changed gate were not run due missing credentials/certificate and unavailable Crabbox/Testbox tooling.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Provider registration/auth metadata, static catalog, business endpoint config, onboarding defaults, GigaChat rate/context classification, OAuth token request shape, TLS/private-network passthrough, token caching, concurrent refresh deduplication, and token endpoint error surfacing.
- Edge cases checked: Pasted Authorization key with and without `Basic ` prefix, invalid/missing token response, scoped OAuth body, protected header override filtering, and cached token reuse before refresh margin.
- What you did **not** verify: Real Sber OAuth token exchange, real chat completion, real certificate trust path, and broad Testbox/Crabbox changed gate.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: New users can run `openclaw onboard --auth-choice gigachat-authorization-key` or set `GIGACHAT_AUTHORIZATION_KEY`; optional config lives under `plugins.entries.gigachat.config.scope` and `plugins.entries.gigachat.config.endpoint`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: GigaChat live TLS/certificate setup can fail on machines that do not trust the required Russian root certificate.
  - Mitigation: Docs recommend provider-scoped `models.providers.gigachat.request.tls.ca` so the Russian root certificate applies only to GigaChat OAuth/chat requests; OS trust and `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` are documented as process-wide fallbacks, and `insecureSkipVerify` is discouraged outside controlled development.
- Risk: GigaChat multimodal attachments and function calling differ from OpenAI-compatible payloads.
  - Mitigation: Initial catalog is text-only and explicitly disables tool/function support until a dedicated adapter is implemented and tested.
- Risk: OAuth token exchange could create excess token endpoint calls.
  - Mitigation: Runtime auth caches tokens until shortly before expiry and deduplicates concurrent refreshes.
target.author: remdev

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
